title: Safety Program Coordinator
description:
Incumbent serves as the Post Occupational Safety and Health Officer (POSHO) assistant (Mid-level).

Main Responsibilities:

  • Assists the POSHO and/or senior POSHO Assistant with daily functions of the Office of Safety Health and Environmental Management (SHEM) and Office of Fire Protection (FIRE) programs at post.
  • Ensures all U.S. Government-owned and leased properties in country meet Departmental and other relevant safety requirements.
  • Assists in conducting safety surveys and preparing related reports.
  • Conducts follow-up inspections to ensure compliance with safety standards.
  • Provides safety training in accordance with SHEM requirements.
  • Performs related administrative duties.

Requirements:
All applicants must address each selection criterion detailed below with specific and comprehensive information supporting each item in their application. Failure to do so may result in a determination that the applicant is not qualified.

  • Minimum two years post-Secondary studies at the College/University level or a two-year Vocational training in facilities management, environmental safety, construction, engineering, or architecture.
  • At least 3 years of experience in the maintenance or construction field performing repair work as a maintenance supervisor or team lead or in the field of occupational safety as a safety inspector or quality control assurance inspector required.
  • Fluent (speaking/reading/writing) in English is required.
  • Good understanding of local occupational safety and health regulations.
  • Knowledge of local construction policies and local labor laws.
  • In depth knowledge of occupational safety and health standards, safety regulations, industry best practices, methods of assessing safety hazards and controls.
  • Knowledge of investigative techniques for conducting investigations for all types of mishaps.
  • Good knowledge of local building codes.
  • Proficiency in maintaining safety and health records, including incident reports, inspection records, and training documentation.  Strong communication skills to effectively convey safety information, policies, and procedures to employees and management.  
  • Knowledge of Auto CAD and/or Revit. MS Project.
  • A valid Class 3 driver’s license for at least five years is required.

Additional job details can be requested by emailing USEmbSingaporeHR@state.gov

Please note:

  • Emailed applications and resumes are not accepted.
  • To maintain an open and fair recruitment process, the U.S. Embassy only accepts applications that are completed in full and submitted via the Electronic Recruitment Application (ERA) website: https://erajobs.state.gov/dos-era/sgp/vacancysearch/searchVacancies.hms
  • To apply for the above position: Please complete the on-line ERA form and upload any supporting documents (including educational certificates and/or transcripts related to the position requirements state above) using this site https://erajobs.state.gov/dos-era/sgp/vacancysearch/searchVacancies.hms by February 10, 2026.
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Thank you for your interest in U.S. Embassy Singapore.
